fr.elements: Main Building; Access Road; Vehicle Parking Area; Perimeter Fencing; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: Ensuring that multiple access points are clearly marked and easily navigable could improve emergency responder access and streamline movement across the property. Regular maintenance of the access road and vehicle parking area may enhance vehicle maneuverability and support staging operations. Clear delineation and potential removable sections of the perimeter fencing could facilitate entry and egress for emergency responders. Additionally, maintaining trimmed and strategically placed landscaping along routes to the main building may aid visibility and navigation, ensuring that emergency operations are conducted smoothly and efficiently.

wildfire.conditions: Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Scattered Trees; Scattered Shrubs; Defensible Space; Open Fuel Break; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Vegetated Drainage
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by managing the vegetated perimeter around the property, especially focusing on the scattered trees and shrubs, may help reduce fuel continuity and improve fire resilience. Maintaining and potentially expanding the existing open terrain and fuel break areas can further impede fire spread and support long-term preparedness. Regular management of vegetation adjacent to the access route is crucial to facilitate safe firefighter access and support suppression activities. Efforts to manage vegetation within and around the vegetated drainage could also help in maintaining these areas as effective barriers against fire encroachment. Collectively, these actions could bolster wildfire mitigation efforts and contribute to the overall safety and resilience of the property.
